evals.prompt: Let's start the delete account endpoint (§B-3). What stage are we on and what should we do first?
evals.expected_output: AI reads e2e-status.md, project-map.md, route-map.md, confirms §B-3 is ⏳, identifies the correct package (profile/me/), and produces a Stage 0 design doc with HTTP contract, guard ordering, and complete test case inventory.
expectations: Reads e2e-status.md to confirm DELETE /profile/me is ⏳ (not yet started)
Identifies the Go package as internal/domain/profile/me/ from route-map.md
Produces Stage 0 with §2 HTTP contract including all error codes (401, 413, 422, 429, 500)
§5 guard ordering lists: MaxBytesReader → extract userID → service call → cookie clear → audit
§7 test case inventory covers S-layer, H-layer, and I-layer cases derived mechanically from guard ordering
Saves the design doc to docs/prompts/delete-account/0-design.md
Writes context.md immediately after saving the design doc

evals.prompt: I'm starting the Google OAuth feature (§D-1). Generate stage 0.
evals.expected_output: AI reads e2e-status.md (confirms D-1 ⏳), reads route-map.md (internal/domain/auth/oauth/google/), reads INCOMING.md §D-1 only, reads closest analogous handler+service, produces full Stage 0 including PKCE decisions, state CSRF, cookie security, audit events, and complete T-NN test inventory.
expectations: Reads only INCOMING.md §D-1, not the full file
Identifies package as internal/domain/auth/oauth/google/ from route-map.md
§3 decisions table covers PKCE integrity, state parameter CSRF, cookie flags, token encryption (D-xx entries)
§4 lists new audit events using Event{PastTense} naming convention
§5 guard ordering covers initiate, callback, link, and unlink handler methods
§7 test inventory includes timing invariant cases and context.WithoutCancel cases for audit writes
Does not read RULES.md in full — only the §3.13 checklist section and relevant §3.x rules

evals.prompt: We finished stage 3 for delete-account. Continue.
evals.expected_output: AI lists docs/prompts/delete-account/, finds 0-design.md through 3-logic.md exist, checks whether internal/domain/profile/me/service.go has the Delete method implemented, finds it does, produces Stage 4 HTTP layer prompt and saves it.
expectations: Lists docs/prompts/delete-account/ to find highest stage prompt (3-logic.md)
Checks internal/domain/profile/me/service.go for Delete method implementation
Does NOT silently assume which stage to produce — verifies implementation exists first
Produces Stage 4 prompt with handler template, routes template, and H+I layer test cases from §7
Saves to docs/prompts/delete-account/4-http.md
Immediately produces Stage 5 audit prompt and saves to docs/prompts/delete-account/5-audit.md

evals.prompt: Write stage 2 for the admin user list endpoint (§F-1a).
evals.expected_output: AI reads orient files, confirms admin domain does not yet exist, reads project-map.md §1 for analogous store pattern, produces Stage 2 with Store struct shape, WithQuerier, FakeStorer entry in authsharedtest, QuerierProxy Fail* fields — all following exact conventions.
expectations: Store struct embeds the correct shared BaseStore for the admin domain
var _ Storer = (*Store)(nil) compile-time check present in store.go deliverable
WithQuerier method present with correct copy-and-replace pattern
FakeStorer struct named AdminUsersFakeStorer (or equivalent) added to shared/testutil/fake_storer.go
QuerierProxy Fail* fields added under a new // ── admin users ── section separator
No pgtype.* in any public Store method signatures in the deliverable
Done-when checklist includes: go build ./... passes, var _ db.Querier = (*QuerierProxy)(nil) still passes

evals.prompt: Stage 5 audit for change-password. The package is internal/domain/auth/password/.
evals.expected_output: AI produces a 4-part audit prompt: Part 1 Security (timing invariant, bcrypt-in-tx prohibition, cookie flags), Part 2 Go Engineer (guard ordering per 0-design.md §5, error wrapping, WithoutCancel), Part 3 Platform Compliance (respond.*, token.*, ratelimit.*), Part 4 Test Coverage ([x]/[❌] table of every T-NN from §7).
expectations: Exactly 4 parts in order — no extra sections
Part 2 guard-ordering sub-sections copy guard steps verbatim from 0-design.md §5
Part 3 platform table has a row for every platform helper the change-password handler uses
Part 4 T-NN table copies every H-layer test case from 0-design.md §7 with [x] or [❌] status
Sync checklist at the end lists all 5 required items (Part 1 Critical/High, Part 2 deviations, Part 3 violations, Part 4 missing tests, go build/vet/test)
Does not produce a flat checklist or single-perspective review
